summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Kyrios appreciation page. All core features working correctly including hero section, quotes, rotating facts, achievements grid, intel report, thank you section, scroll animations, and navigation. Minor issue with OG meta tags due to duplicate tags from index.html.

issues: Duplicate OG meta tags: index.html has default meta tags that are not replaced by react-helmet-async, resulting in duplicate og:title, og:description, twitter:card tags. Discord/social crawlers will read the first (default) tags instead of Kyrios-specific ones. This is a known limitation of client-side rendering with react-helmet-async.

Title conflict: useFavicon.js sets title to '⚔️ Kyrios | TW Appreciation' which overrides Helmet's 'Kyrios — Top War Discord Moderator'. This is intentional behavior per useFavicon.js mapping but differs from Helmet specification.

passed_tests: Hero section with samurai avatar displayed correctly
KYRIOS name with shimmer animation displayed
Main quote card displays full team quote
Two additional quote cards visible ('Always helpful...' and 'Positive team player')
Rotating fun facts card with emerald accent - facts rotate every 4 seconds
6 achievement badge cards displayed (Bug Hunter, Community Pillar, Team Player, Great Asset, Event Ready, Always Online)
4 Intel Report cards displayed (Bug Detection Specialist, Event Veteran, The Samurai Spirit, Knowledge Base Walking)
Back to Login button navigates to /moderator/login
Scroll-triggered reveal animations working (16/16 elements gain 'visible' class)
All 26 data-testid attributes present and unique
Favicon mapping exists for /kyrios-appreciation in useFavicon.js (/favicons/kyrios.svg)
OG meta tags present via react-helmet-async (og:title, og:description, og:image, twitter:card) - but duplicated with index.html defaults
action_items: OPTIONAL: To fix OG meta tags for Discord embeds, either: (1) Remove default OG meta tags from index.html and let each page set its own via Helmet, OR (2) Implement server-side rendering for proper meta tag injection. Current implementation works for client-side but social crawlers will see default tags.
critical_code_review_comments: KyriosPage.js follows the same pattern as GarudaPage.js and SethPage.js - consistent implementation
useFavicon.js correctly maps /kyrios-appreciation to favicon and title
App.js route correctly configured at line 99
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Kyrios appreciation page fully tested and working. OG meta tag duplication is a known limitation of react-helmet-async with client-side rendering - not a bug but a design consideration for social sharing. All interactive elements, animations, and navigation working correctly.
